employed.title: Employed
employed.summary: Your timeline focuses on permit compliance, payroll, and settling in.

job_seeking.title: Looking for work
job_seeking.summary: Prioritise permit rules, networking, and Intreo registration where eligible.
tips: Check whether your current stamp still allows job seeking — rules differ by permit type.
Register with Intreo (social protection) if you are legally permitted to seek work.
Use Community forums and Members to connect with hiring managers in your city.
Keep proof of job-search activity if your permit renewal depends on employment.

unemployed.title: Unemployed
unemployed.summary: We'll highlight permit, welfare, and job-search steps tailored to your situation.
tips: Contact your employer or immigration adviser about how unemployment affects your stamp.
Register with Intreo if you are eligible for job-seeker supports in Ireland.
Review permit renewal timelines — gaps in employment can affect General Work Permits.
Message mentors in Members or ask the AI guide about PPS, welfare, and next steps.

laid_off.title: Redundant / laid off
laid_off.summary: Losing your job abroad is stressful — track permit deadlines and support options early.
tips: Request a redundancy letter from HR — you may need it for immigration or welfare.
Speak to immigration about how redundancy affects your IRP stamp and renewal.
You may have a limited window to find a new role under the same permit category.
Use Journey → Timeline for urgent admin tasks and Community for peer advice.
